title: BioScientist Agent: Designing LLM-Biomedical Agents with KG-Augmented RL Reasoning Modules for Drug Repurposing and Mechanistic of Action Elucidation
abstract: Drug discovery is protracted, resource-intensive, and afflicted by attrition rates exceeding 90 %, which leaves most diseases, particularly rare or neglected indications, without effective therapies. Drug repurposing offers a cost effective alternative, yet systematic identification of novel drug indication pairs and mechanistic rationales remains hindered by the scale and heterogeneity of biomedical knowledge. We present BioScientist Agent , an end to end framework that unifies a billion-fact biomedical knowledge graph with (i) a variational graph auto-encoder for representation learning and link prediction driven drug repurposing, (ii) a reinforcement learning module that traverses the graph to recover biologically plausible mechanistic paths, and (iii) A large language model (LLM) multi-agent layer that orchestrates these components, enabling inference of target pathways for a drug disease pair, and automatic generation of coherent causal reports. In all downstream tasks, the BioScientist Agent surpasses existing state of the art baseline models across various metrics and provides mechanistic explanations consistent with the literature. Its open and modular design accelerates hypothesis generation and reduces experimental overhead in early stage discovery.

paper_title: BioScientist Agent: Designing LLM-Biomedical Agents with KG-Augmented RL Reasoning Modules for Drug Repurposing and Mechanistic of Action Elucidation

objective.answer: The primary objective of the paper is to develop the BioScientist Agent, a framework that integrates a biomedical knowledge graph with machine learning models to facilitate drug repurposing and elucidate mechanisms of action.
objective.evidence: We present BioScientist Agent, an end to end framework that unifies a billion-fact biomedical knowledge graph with (i) a variational graph auto-encoder for representation learning and link prediction driven drug repurposing, (ii) a reinforcement learning module that traverses the graph to recover biologically plausible mechanistic paths, and (iii) A large language model (LLM) multi-agent layer that orchestrates these components, enabling inference of target pathways for a drug disease pair, and automatic generation of coherent causal reports.

knowledge_gap.answer: The paper addresses the challenge of systematically identifying novel drug indication pairs and mechanistic rationales, which is hindered by the scale and heterogeneity of biomedical knowledge.
knowledge_gap.evidence: Drug repurposing offers a cost effective alternative, yet systematic identification of novel drug indication pairs and mechanistic rationales remains hindered by the scale and heterogeneity of biomedical knowledge.

answer: Integration of a variational graph auto-encoder for state-of-the-art drug-disease link prediction.
Development of an adversarial actor-critic reinforcement-learning algorithm for discovering biologically plausible pathways.
Creation of an LLM-driven multi-agent system for pathway elucidation and automated causal report generation.
evidence: We curate and preprocess RTX-KG2 (v2.9.2) to obtain a 6.3 million-node, 41 million-edge biomedical KG and train a variational graph auto-encoder (VGAE) that achieves state-of-the-art performance on drug–disease link-prediction benchmarks.

We devise an adversarial actor–critic reinforcement-learning algorithm that discovers biologically plausible drug–target–disease pathways, yielding interpretable mechanistic explanations for each prediction.

We integrate these models within an LLM-driven multi-agent system supporting four practical workflows: (i) disease to drug search or drug to disease search, (ii) pathway elucidation and (iii) automated causal report generation.

inspirational_papers.evidence: We apply a reinforcement learning model ADAC to learn the correct pathways from drugs to diseases [ZWZ+20].

evaluation_metrics.name: Mean Reciprocal Rank (MRR)
evaluation_metrics.purpose: Measures the average rank of the true pair across all queries.
evaluation_metrics.application: Used to assess overall ranking quality in drug repurposing tasks.
evaluation_metrics.evidence: MRR assesses overall ranking quality by calculating the average reciprocal rank of the true pair across all queries.

evaluation_metrics.name: Hit@K
evaluation_metrics.purpose: Measures the proportion of true pairs appearing in the top K candidates.
evaluation_metrics.application: Provides a top-K recommendation performance metric.
evaluation_metrics.evidence: Hits@K measures the proportion of true pairs appearing in the top K candidates.

methods.name: Hypothesis or Idea Generation
methods.description: The system produces candidate hypotheses or new research ideas from prior knowledge or external input.
methods.evidence: This approach will automate the generation of drug mechanisms of action and enhance interpretability.

methods.name: Experimental design generation
methods.description: The approach includes producing experimental protocols, configurations, or evaluation strategies.
methods.evidence: The system comprises of Perception, Brain, and Action components. Perception is responsible for acquiring pathway information from the ADAC module.

performance_summary.summary: The BioScientist Agent outperformed existing high-performance algorithms in drug repurposing and MoA pathway prediction tasks.
performance_summary.evidence: In the drug repurposing task, compared to existing high performance algorithms, including GraphSage, GAT, TAGConv, GCN, TransformerConv, and SGFormer, the BioScientist Agent utilizing VGAE demonstrated superior performance.

baselines.evidence: In the drug repurposing task, compared to existing high performance algorithms, including GraphSage, GAT, TAGConv, GCN, TransformerConv, and SGFormer, the BioScientist Agent utilizing VGAE demonstrated superior performance.

evaluation_metrics.name: Accuracy
evaluation_metrics.purpose: Measures the proportion of correct predictions.
evaluation_metrics.application: Used to evaluate the performance of the BioScientist Agent in drug repurposing tasks.
evaluation_metrics.evidence: In the drug repurposing task, compared to existing high performance algorithms, including GraphSage, GAT, TAGConv, GCN, TransformerConv, and SGFormer, the BioScientist Agent utilizing VGAE demonstrated superior performance, improving accuracy and F1 score by approximately 3.3% to 3.7%.

evaluation_metrics.name: F1 Score
evaluation_metrics.purpose: Measures the balance between precision and recall.
evaluation_metrics.application: Used to evaluate the performance of the BioScientist Agent in drug repurposing tasks.

limitations.name: Limited Generalizability
limitations.description: The system may not generalize well to other types of biomedical data or knowledge graphs.
limitations.evidence: Currently, the BioScientist Agent can quickly iterate when handling updated databases; however, it still requires retraining from scratch to associate new nodes with existing ones using graph neural network algorithms.

limitations.name: Computational Constraints
limitations.description: Training on large-scale knowledge graphs is computationally intensive.
limitations.evidence: Previous research seldom involved training on such large-scale knowledge graphs due to computational constraints.

future_directions.name: Rapid Integration of New Nodes
future_directions.description: Establish mechanisms for the rapid integration of new nodes and edge relationships.
future_directions.evidence: In our future work, we aim to establish mechanisms for the rapid integration of new nodes and edge relationships.

future_directions.name: Enhance Node Representation
future_directions.description: Incorporate more node representation models to enhance their representation abilities.
future_directions.evidence: This includes updating nodes through local graph representation capabilities and incorporating more node representation models to enhance their representation abilities.
